name: certipy-ad
title: certipy-ad

commands.name: certipy-ad

arguments.description: Show Certipy's version number and exit
arguments.raw: -v, --version         Show Certipy's version number and exit

arguments.description: Show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            Show this help message and exit

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# certipy-ad -h
 usage: certipy-ad [-v] [-h]
                 {account,auth,ca,cert,find,parse,forge,relay,req,shadow,template} ...
 
 Active Directory Certificate Services enumeration and abuse
 
 positional arguments:
 {account,auth,ca,cert,find,parse,forge,relay,req,shadow,template}
                       Action
   account             Manage user and machine accounts
   auth                Authenticate using certificates
   ca                  Manage CA and certificates
   cert                Manage certificates and private keys
   find                Enumerate AD CS
   parse               Offline enumerate AD CS based on registry data
   forge               Create Golden Certificates or self-signed certificates
   relay               NTLM Relay to AD CS HTTP Endpoints
   req                 Request certificates
   shadow              Abuse Shadow Credentials for account takeover
   template            Manage certificate templates
 
 options:
 -v, --version         Show Certipy's version number and exit
 -h, --help            Show this help message and exit

apt_metadata.depends: python3-argcomplete, python3-asn1crypto, python3-bs4 (>= 4.13.4), python3-cryptography (>= 42.0.8), python3-dnspython, python3-httpx, python3-impacket, python3-ldap3, python3-pyasn1 (>= 0.6.1), python3-pycryptodome, python3-requests, python3:any, python3-openssl
apt_metadata.description:
Tool for attacking AD Certificate Services
Offensive tool for enumerating and abusing Active
Directory Certificate Services (AD CS).

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.

Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
